input_value.statement: Waiting lists can be cleared within one parliamentary term without funding well beyond current manifesto commitments
Waiting lists can be cleared within one parliamentary term without funding well beyond current manifesto commitments
NHS funding at manifesto-pledged levels is sufficient to deliver the Long Term Workforce Plan and restore performance standards
NHS funding at manifesto-pledged levels is sufficient to deliver the Long Term Workforce Plan and restore performance standards
The NHS Long Term Workforce Plan can deliver its staffing targets without continued reliance on overseas recruitment for the next 15 years
The NHS Long Term Workforce Plan can deliver its staffing targets without continued reliance on overseas recruitment for the next 15 years
Social care reform will be delivered by the current government despite every government for the past 25 years promising and failing to do so
Social care reform will be delivered by the current government despite every government for the past 25 years promising and failing to do so
Technology and AI adoption can close the NHS productivity gap without addressing the underlying capital investment shortfall
Technology and AI adoption can close the NHS productivity gap without addressing the underlying capital investment shortfall
Shifting NHS spending from hospitals to community care and prevention would be more cost-effective than the current hospital-dominated model
Shifting NHS spending from hospitals to community care and prevention would be more cost-effective than the current hospital-dominated model
Expanding private healthcare through tax relief would reduce pressure on the NHS at a system level
Expanding private healthcare through tax relief would reduce pressure on the NHS at a system level
Implement the NHS Long Term Workforce Plan with 92,000 more nurses and 28,000 more doctors by end of parliament, while reducing managers by 5,500
Provide an extra £3.7 billion per year for social care and cap individual care costs
Invest £3.4 billion in NHS AI and technology and deliver the New Hospitals Programme
Provide 40,000 extra NHS appointments per week, deliver the Long Term Workforce Plan, and create a National Care Service with a Fair Pay Agreement for social care workers
Reform the Mental Health Act, deploy the NHS App as the front door to services, and shift towards prevention with a 10-year plan
Invest an extra £8.4 billion per year in health and social care by 2028/29, recruit 8,000 more GPs, and guarantee cancer treatment within 62 days
Deliver free personal care, a carer's minimum wage at £2 per hour above standard, an extra £1 billion per year for public health, and establish a Royal College of Care Workers

Invest £20 billion in free personal care, restore public health budgets with an extra £1.5 billion per year, and address social determinants of health by ending the distinction between health and social care
Invest an extra £17 billion per year in the NHS, eliminate waiting lists within two years, and provide tax relief on private healthcare to reduce NHS demand
Immigrants are net fiscal contributors to the UK economy
Immigrants are net fiscal contributors to the UK economy
Immigration significantly suppresses wages for British workers
Immigration significantly suppresses wages for British workers
Numerical caps on immigration reduce net migration effectively
Numerical caps on immigration reduce net migration effectively
ECHR membership prevents the UK from deporting foreign criminals and controlling immigration at scale
ECHR membership prevents the UK from deporting foreign criminals and controlling immigration at scale
Establishing safe legal routes for refugees would reduce irregular Channel crossings
Establishing safe legal routes for refugees would reduce irregular Channel crossings
Deterrence through offshore processing or deportation flights effectively reduces irregular migration
Deterrence through offshore processing or deportation flights effectively reduces irregular migration
Fast asylum processing is cheaper than maintaining the backlog
Fast asylum processing is cheaper than maintaining the backlog
Immigration is the primary cause of pressure on public services such as the NHS, housing, and education
Immigration is the primary cause of pressure on public services such as the NHS, housing, and education
Successful integration of immigrants depends primarily on language provision, employment access, and speed of processing
Successful integration of immigrants depends primarily on language provision, employment access, and speed of processing
Zero net migration is an economically viable target for the UK
Zero net migration is an economically viable target for the UK
Impose a binding annual cap on work and family visas set by MAC recommendations and voted annually by Parliament, with the cap decreasing each year
Continue the Rwanda deportation scheme with regular flights, bring the Illegal Migration Act into full force blocking all asylum processing, and clear the asylum backlog within 6 months
Raise salary thresholds for skilled workers to £38,700, ban student and care worker dependants, and introduce health checks for visa applicants

Reduce net migration without committing to a specific numerical target, and link immigration policy to skills strategy via a strengthened MAC requiring high-visa sectors to produce domestic training plans

Scrap the Rwanda scheme and establish a Border Security Command with counter-terror powers, deploy 1,000-staff returns and enforcement unit, and pursue EU intelligence-sharing agreements for Channel security
Maintained and raised Conservative salary thresholds to £41,700 and closed the overseas care worker recruitment route to new applicants in July 2025
End the hostile environment, transfer work visa policy out of the Home Office to an economic department, establish safe legal routes for refugees, and create a UK-EU youth mobility scheme

Set a 3-month processing standard for asylum claims, scrap the Illegal Migration Act, reverse dependant bans for care workers, and establish a firewall preventing public agencies sharing data with the Home Office

Pursue a long-term aspiration toward a world without borders, introduce automatic 3-month visitor visas, remove minimum income requirements, establish birthright citizenship, and end all immigration detention

Leave the ECHR, achieve zero net migration, freeze non-essential immigration, establish a UK Deportation Command with 24,000 detention capacity and 5 deportation flights per day, and impose employer NI surcharge on foreign hires
The UK can build 300,000 homes per year under current policy settings including planning reform alone
The UK can build 300,000 homes per year under current policy settings including planning reform alone
The planning system is the main barrier to increased housebuilding in the UK
The planning system is the main barrier to increased housebuilding in the UK
Immigration is the primary driver of the UK housing crisis
Immigration is the primary driver of the UK housing crisis
Right to Buy has been beneficial for the UK housing system overall
Right to Buy has been beneficial for the UK housing system overall
Rent controls are an effective solution to the UK's housing affordability crisis
Rent controls are an effective solution to the UK's housing affordability crisis
The private sector alone can deliver housing at the scale needed to close the UK's housing deficit
The private sector alone can deliver housing at the scale needed to close the UK's housing deficit
Building significantly more homes will reduce housing prices and improve affordability
Building significantly more homes will reduce housing prices and improve affordability
The quality and energy efficiency of the UK's existing housing stock is in crisis
The quality and energy efficiency of the UK's existing housing stock is in crisis
Build 1.6 million homes over the parliament without mandatory local targets, prioritise brownfield development, extend Help to Buy and Mortgage Guarantee Scheme, and maintain Right to Buy

Build 1.5 million homes with restored mandatory local targets, planning reform via NPPF update, New Towns programme, release grey belt land with 50% affordable, and deliver the £39bn Social and Affordable Homes Programme
Build 380,000 homes per year including 150,000 social homes, create ten new garden cities, abolish residential leasehold, end Right to Buy locally, and restore EPC C minimum for landlords
Build 150,000 social homes per year, invest £29bn over 5 years to raise all homes to EPC B, introduce rent controls, and allow councils to requisition empty or poorly insulated properties

Fast-track brownfield planning with tax incentives, prioritise local people for social housing with foreign nationals moved to the back of the queue, scrap the Renters Reform Bill, and state no housing targets
Tax cuts can pay for themselves through increased economic growth at current UK tax levels
Tax cuts can pay for themselves through increased economic growth at current UK tax levels
Closing the tax gap can generate enough revenue to fund manifesto spending commitments
Closing the tax gap can generate enough revenue to fund manifesto spending commitments
An annual wealth tax on high-value assets is feasible and administrable in the UK
An annual wealth tax on high-value assets is feasible and administrable in the UK
Capital Gains Tax should be equalised with income tax rates to remove the incentive to reclassify income as capital gains
Capital Gains Tax should be equalised with income tax rates to remove the incentive to reclassify income as capital gains
Council tax reform replacing 1991-based bands with a proportional property tax is overdue and justified
Council tax reform replacing 1991-based bands with a proportional property tax is overdue and justified
The UK can fund its public services at the level the evidence requires without raising income tax, National Insurance, or VAT
The UK can fund its public services at the level the evidence requires without raising income tax, National Insurance, or VAT

Cut employee NI by a further 2p to 6% by 2027, abolish self-employed NI entirely, cap corporation tax at 25%, fund through £12 billion in welfare savings, and pledge no rises to income tax, NI, VAT, or corporation tax

Pledge no rises to income tax rates, NI, VAT, or corporation tax, raise £1.7 billion from VAT on private schools, abolish non-dom status, target £5 billion from closing tax avoidance, and fund spending through economic growth
Reverse bank levy and surcharge cuts to raise £8.7 billion by 2028-29, increase Digital Services Tax to 6%, introduce a sewage tax on water company profits, and target £7.2 billion from the tax gap

Introduce a wealth tax of 1% above £10 million and 2% above £1 billion, equalise CGT with income tax, restrict pension tax relief to basic rate, introduce a carbon tax, and fund £40 billion per year in green investment

Cut £50 billion per year in government spending, abolish IHT on estates under £2 million, raise income tax threshold to £20,000, cut corporation tax and fuel duty, scrap VAT on energy, and fund through spending cuts and scrapping net zero
The two-child benefit limit reduces poverty or incentivises work sufficiently to justify its impact on children
The two-child benefit limit reduces poverty or incentivises work sufficiently to justify its impact on children
£12 billion in welfare savings can be achieved through tightening eligibility and increased sanctions as the Conservatives proposed
£12 billion in welfare savings can be achieved through tightening eligibility and increased sanctions as the Conservatives proposed
Universal Basic Income is feasible at meaningful levels in the UK
Universal Basic Income is feasible at meaningful levels in the UK
Getting people into work is sufficient to solve poverty in the UK
Getting people into work is sufficient to solve poverty in the UK
The rise in disability benefit claims is driven by fraud rather than genuine increases in long-term sickness
The rise in disability benefit claims is driven by fraud rather than genuine increases in long-term sickness
The benefit cap effectively reduces poverty or welfare dependency rather than simply shifting costs elsewhere
The benefit cap effectively reduces poverty or welfare dependency rather than simply shifting costs elsewhere

Maintain the two-child limit, achieve £12 billion in welfare savings through tightened disability benefits and increased sanctions, and reduce welfare rolls through conditionality while protecting the pension triple lock

Remove the two-child limit from April 2026 via legislation, develop a child poverty strategy, review Universal Credit, and launch the Get Britain Working plan tackling NHS waits, mental health, and Access to Work alongside a Fair Pay Agreement for social care

Remove the two-child limit and benefit cap, end deep poverty within a decade, establish an independent UC adequacy commission, introduce a carer's minimum wage at £2 per hour above standard, and expand Carer's Allowance eligibility

Pilot Universal Basic Income, remove all benefit caps and limits, restore benefits to pre-austerity levels, end the sanctions regime entirely, and fund £20 billion in free personal care through wealth taxes

Cut £50 billion in government spending, incentivise employment through tax cuts rather than welfare reform, redirect overseas aid to domestic welfare, and prioritise British citizens for social housing and benefits
School funding at current levels is adequate to maintain or improve educational outcomes
School funding at current levels is adequate to maintain or improve educational outcomes
Closing low-quality university courses can fund 100,000 apprenticeships as the Conservatives proposed
Closing low-quality university courses can fund 100,000 apprenticeships as the Conservatives proposed
The Growth and Skills Levy will improve skills provision by broadening the apprenticeship levy
The Growth and Skills Levy will improve skills provision by broadening the apprenticeship levy
University tuition fees should be scrapped and replaced with free higher education funded through taxation
University tuition fees should be scrapped and replaced with free higher education funded through taxation
Early years investment produces the highest return on investment in the entire education system
Early years investment produces the highest return on investment in the entire education system
Ofsted should be abolished rather than reformed to address its harmful effects on schools and teachers
Ofsted should be abolished rather than reformed to address its harmful effects on schools and teachers
The teacher workforce crisis can be solved through recruitment targets alone without addressing pay and conditions
The teacher workforce crisis can be solved through recruitment targets alone without addressing pay and conditions

Protect day-to-day school spending per pupil in real terms, close low-quality university courses to fund 100,000 apprenticeships, introduce National Service for school leavers, and ban mobile phones in schools

Recruit 6,500 new teachers in shortage subjects, reform Ofsted by ending single-word judgements, broaden the apprenticeship levy into a Growth and Skills Levy, establish 3,000 nurseries in schools, and fund state education via VAT on private schools

Increase school and college funding above inflation annually, reform the Teachers' Review Body for independent pay, provide a £5,000 Lifelong Skills Grant for all adults, reintroduce maintenance grants, and rejoin Erasmus
Fully fund all HE students by restoring grants and scrapping tuition fees, cancel graduate debt, abolish Ofsted, end formal assessments, and deliver universal free school meals
Scrap interest on student loans, restrict undergraduate numbers, enforce minimum entry standards, cut funding to universities undermining free speech, and implement a patriotic curriculum
Renewables are unambiguously cheaper than nuclear power for UK electricity generation
Renewables are unambiguously cheaper than nuclear power for UK electricity generation
The UK electricity grid can be fully decarbonised by 2030
The UK electricity grid can be fully decarbonised by 2030
Fracking is a viable energy source for the UK
Fracking is a viable energy source for the UK
Renewable energy in the UK is dependent on subsidies and would collapse without them
Renewable energy in the UK is dependent on subsidies and would collapse without them
New North Sea oil and gas extraction is compatible with the UK's net zero commitments
New North Sea oil and gas extraction is compatible with the UK's net zero commitments
Reducing CO2 emissions cannot help address climate change
Reducing CO2 emissions cannot help address climate change
The net employment impact of the energy transition is negative for the UK
The net employment impact of the energy transition is negative for the UK

Maintain net zero 2050 pragmatically with annual North Sea licensing, new gas plants, two SMR fleets, treble offshore wind but restrict onshore wind and solar, reduce green levies, and require parliamentary votes before new carbon targets

Achieve clean power by 2030, create Great British Energy as a public company, double onshore wind, triple solar, quadruple offshore wind, no new North Sea licences, windfall tax on oil and gas, and double home insulation funding

Achieve net zero by 2045, invest in renewables and nuclear, launch an emergency home insulation programme, ban fracking and new coal, promote community energy, and invest in tidal, wave, hydrogen, pumped storage and battery technologies

Achieve carbon neutrality before 2040, phase out nuclear entirely, generate 70% from wind by 2030, cancel existing fossil fuel licences, invest £40 billion per year funded by wealth taxes, and take an insulation-first community-owned approach

Scrap net zero entirely along with all renewable subsidies, fast-track North Sea oil and gas, enable fracking, fast-track SMRs, scrap VAT on energy, explore coal mining, and claim CO2 reduction cannot help climate change
The 2.5% of GDP defence spending target is sufficient for UK security given the current threat environment
The 2.5% of GDP defence spending target is sufficient for UK security given the current threat environment
Defence spending increases can be funded by cutting overseas development aid without creating new security risks
Defence spending increases can be funded by cutting overseas development aid without creating new security risks
The nuclear deterrent remains the appropriate strategic insurance and should be maintained at current scale
The nuclear deterrent remains the appropriate strategic insurance and should be maintained at current scale
The capital-heavy investment bias toward new equipment rather than personnel and readiness is the right balance for UK defence
The capital-heavy investment bias toward new equipment rather than personnel and readiness is the right balance for UK defence
Defence spending can increase to 3.5% of GDP without squeezing other public services or raising taxes
Defence spending can increase to 3.5% of GDP without squeezing other public services or raising taxes
Reach 2.5% by 2030, maintain the nuclear deterrent, pursue AUKUS, deliver new equipment programmes, and introduce National Service for school leavers
Accelerate to 2.5% by 2027 and 3% next parliament via the externally-led Strategic Defence Review, fund partly by cutting aid from 0.5% to 0.3% of GNI, maintain nuclear deterrent and AUKUS
Commit to 2.5% of GDP, review the nuclear deterrent rather than committing to like-for-like Trident replacement, and invest in cyber and resilience alongside maintaining NATO commitment
Set no defence spending target, oppose increases, and redirect military spending to public services and climate action with no mention of NATO targets
Increase military spending with a strong defence posture, introduce National Service, and take a militarised approach to border enforcement
Brexit has been economically damaging to the UK
Brexit has been economically damaging to the UK
Free trade agreements with non-EU countries can compensate for lost EU single market access
Free trade agreements with non-EU countries can compensate for lost EU single market access
Labour's reset with the EU is sufficient to recover the economic damage from Brexit
Labour's reset with the EU is sufficient to recover the economic damage from Brexit
A customs union with the EU would be net beneficial for the UK despite losing independent trade policy
A customs union with the EU would be net beneficial for the UK despite losing independent trade policy
Freedom of movement is the binding political constraint preventing UK economic recovery through closer EU integration
Freedom of movement is the binding political constraint preventing UK economic recovery through closer EU integration
Maintain the Brexit settlement with no closer EU alignment, pursue independent trade policy through CPTPP and new global FTAs
Reset with EU within red lines of no single market, no customs union, and no freedom of movement, pursue sector-specific alignment while maintaining that closer alignment is preferable
Pursue a bespoke UK-EU customs union as stepping stone with long-term objective of rejoining single market, implement UK-EU youth mobility scheme
Rejoin the EU as soon as political will is present
Grasp the opportunities of Brexit, scrap remaining EU-derived legislation, and renegotiate or abandon post-Brexit UK-EU treaties
The UK is on track to meet its legally binding environmental targets set in the Environment Act 2021
The UK is on track to meet its legally binding environmental targets set in the Environment Act 2021
Sewage pollution can be addressed through regulation alone without structural reform of water companies
Sewage pollution can be addressed through regulation alone without structural reform of water companies
Farming can be both productive and nature-friendly if the transition is properly funded
Farming can be both productive and nature-friendly if the transition is properly funded
Abolishing nutrient neutrality rules would unlock housing without significant environmental damage
Abolishing nutrient neutrality rules would unlock housing without significant environmental damage
Nature has quantifiable economic value that makes environmental protection economically rational
Nature has quantifiable economic value that makes environmental protection economically rational

Increase the agriculture budget by £1 billion, ring-fence farming budget, set legally binding food security targets, continue ELM schemes, and abolish nutrient neutrality rules to unlock 100,000 homes
Meet Environment Act targets, develop a land-use framework, make ELM work for farmers and nature, protect green belt with grey belt release, and maintain Biodiversity Net Gain
Reform water companies, plant 60 million trees per year, set sewage elimination targets, restore EPC C for landlords, end fuel poverty, and increase regulator funding
Enact a Rights of Nature Act, triple the farming budget for nature-friendly transition, end factory farming, ban harmful pesticides with 50% reduction by 2030, and invest £29 billion in insulation
Scrap net zero and climate-related farming subsidies, return to direct payments, stop Natural England enforcement, drop retained EU environmental laws, fast-track oil and gas, and explore coal mining
FPTP produces better governance than proportional representation systems
FPTP produces better governance than proportional representation systems
Proportional representation would change policy outcomes in the UK toward more evidence-based positions
Proportional representation would change policy outcomes in the UK toward more evidence-based positions
There is sufficient public support for PR to make electoral reform politically achievable
There is sufficient public support for PR to make electoral reform politically achievable
The House of Lords has democratic legitimacy through appointment by elected governments
The House of Lords has democratic legitimacy through appointment by elected governments
Maintain FPTP, voter ID, 18 voting age, and the House of Lords as constituted with no constitutional reform
Extend votes to 16-year-olds, remove hereditary peers, consult on long-term Lords reform, and address voter ID inconsistencies but with no commitment to PR despite 2022 conference vote supporting it
Introduce PR via STV for Westminster and English local government, elect the House of Lords, extend votes to 16, implement automatic voter registration, and cap political donations
Introduce PR for all elections, elect the second chamber, extend votes to 16, implement state funding of parties, and extend the franchise to long-term residents
Hold a referendum on PR, replace the Lords with a smaller elected chamber, introduce direct democracy elements, and impose term limits
Building more prisons will solve the overcrowding and justice crisis
Building more prisons will solve the overcrowding and justice crisis
Tougher sentencing reduces crime and makes the public safer
Tougher sentencing reduces crime and makes the public safer
Legal aid is a luxury that can be cut without systemic consequences
Legal aid is a luxury that can be cut without systemic consequences
The UK's punitive drug policy is more effective than decriminalisation or harm reduction approaches
The UK's punitive drug policy is more effective than decriminalisation or harm reduction approaches
The court backlog can be cleared with current levels of investment and reform
The court backlog can be cleared with current levels of investment and reform

Build four new prisons with 20,000 places, impose tougher sentences for knife crime and grooming and retail assault, continue legal aid at current levels, and mandate life for repeat violent offenders

Review sentencing, create specialist rape courts, extend associate prosecutor powers, restore neighbourhood policing, adopt a public health approach to youth violence, and manage prison capacity through early release with no commitment to increase court or legal aid funding

End prison overcrowding, recruit and retain prison officers, create a Women's Justice Board, replace young offender institutions with secure schools, implement statutory youth diversion, through-the-gate mentorship, and establish a right to affordable legal assistance

Invest £11 billion to restore legal aid and £2.5 billion to repair courts, recruit judges, presume against prison sentences under 2 years, reform drug laws toward a regulated market, and defend the Human Rights Act and ECHR

Implement zero tolerance policing, mandate jail for all violent crime and knife possession, impose mandatory life for drug dealing and repeat violent offenders, deport foreign criminals immediately, and establish high-intensity training camps for young offenders
